input: Check the user profile in the admin panel. Add an at symbol before user and before admin.
ideal: Check the @user profile in the @admin panel.

input: I keep forgetting to use the lru cache decorator. Can you put an at before lru cache?
ideal: I keep forgetting to use the @lru cache decorator.

input: Make sure to ping everyone in general. Tag general with an at symbol and show excitement.
ideal: Make sure to ping everyone in @general!

input: Hey Sarah, check out this new feature. Tag Sarah with an at symbol.
ideal: Hey @Sarah, check out this new feature.

input: The decorator property needs to go above the method. Add an at symbol before property.
ideal: The decorator @property needs to go above the method.

input: We need to update config.yaml and secrets.env. Put an at before each filename.
ideal: We need to update @config.yaml and @secrets.env.

input: Everyone in the engineering channel needs to see this. Tag engineering with an at symbol and make it excited.
ideal: Everyone in the @engineering channel needs to see this!

input: Check the logs in production. Add an at symbol before production.
ideal: Check the logs in @production.

input: I need to update app.py, models.py, and config.py. Put an at before each filename.
ideal: I need to update @app.py, @models.py, and @config.py.

input: Message Sarah about the review. Tag Sarah with an at symbol and show excitement.
ideal: Message @Sarah about the review!

input: The decorator wraps the function. Add an at symbol before decorator. And make it all lowercase.
ideal: the @decorator wraps the function.

input: We need to notify the team. Tag channel with an at symbol. Tag here with an at symbol too. Show excitement.
ideal: We need to notify the team. @channel @here!

input: The username is developer_one. Add an at symbol before developer_one. Type that in lowercase.
ideal: the username is @developer_one.

input: I need to decorate the class with property. Put an at before property and before class.
ideal: I need to decorate the @class with @property.

input: The decorator pattern is really useful. Put an at symbol before decorator.
ideal: The @decorator pattern is really useful.

input: We need to notify everyone in the channel. Tag here and channel with at symbols.
ideal: We need to notify everyone in the @here and @channel.

input: I'm so happy this finally works. Show excitement and tag me with an at symbol.
ideal: I'm so happy this finally works @me!

input: Check the @deprecated decorator in the codebase. Actually wait, I haven't added the at symbol yet. Add it before deprecated.
ideal: Check the @deprecated decorator in the codebase.

input: Please review the pull request. Tag the reviewer with an at symbol, that's Sarah, and also make it sound excited.
ideal: Please review the pull request @Sarah!

input: The config file is settings.yaml. Put an at before the filename, and also before the word config actually.
ideal: The @config file is @settings.yaml.

input: His username is velocity_dev on the platform. Put an at before his handle.
ideal: His username is @velocity_dev on the platform.

input: We need to notify the team in Slack. Tag channel with an at symbol and also tag here.
ideal: We need to notify the team in Slack. @channel and also @here.

input: The configuration file is settings.yaml. Put an at before the filename so they know it's a reference.
ideal: The configuration file is @settings.yaml.

input: The config files are settings.yaml and secrets.env. Add at symbols before both filenames.
ideal: The config files are @settings.yaml and @secrets.env.

input: Check the @deprecated decorator on that method. Wait, no, I mean the @override one. Actually, add an at symbol before override.
ideal: Check the @deprecated decorator on that method. Actually, check the @override one.

input: The decorator pattern uses at symbol before function to wrap it. Add an at symbol before function.
ideal: The decorator pattern uses @function to wrap it.

input: I need to mention john and mary in this post. At symbol before both names please.
ideal: I need to mention @john and @mary in this post.

input: The team channel is where we discuss bugs. Tag channel with an at symbol. And mention the devs too.
ideal: The team @channel is where we discuss bugs. And mention the devs too.

input: The @deprecated annotation warns developers. Wait no I said that wrong. The deprecated annotation warns developers. Add an at symbol before deprecated. And also before override on the next line.
ideal: The @deprecated annotation warns developers. And also @override on the next line.

input: Tell admin and mod to check the logs. Tag admin with an at symbol. Actually tag both admin and mod with at symbols. And while you're at it, put an at before logs too.
ideal: Tell @admin and @mod to check the @logs.

input: The function calculateTotal needs testing. At symbol before calculateTotal. The test file test_calculateTotal dot py exists. Put an at before test_calculateTotal as well. And mention tester Sarah who wrote it. Tag Sarah with an at symbol.
ideal: The function @calculateTotal needs testing. The test file @test_calculateTotal.py exists. And mention tester @Sarah who wrote it.

input: In React useEffect is common but useState is more fundamental. Add an at symbol before useState. Actually I use useReducer more now. Put an at before useReducer. The hook useCallback helps performance. At symbol before useCallback. And my teammate Alex prefers useMemo. Tag Alex with an at symbol. Also put an at before useMemo. Finally mention the library react itself. Put an at before react.
ideal: In React useEffect is common but @useState is more fundamental. Actually I use @useReducer more now. The hook @useCallback helps performance. And my teammate @Alex prefers @useMemo. Finally mention the library @react itself.

input: Send this to Sarah. Put an at symbol before Sarah.
ideal: Send this to @Sarah.

input: The decorator pattern is elegant. Add an at symbol before decorator.
ideal: The @decorator pattern is elegant.

input: I love this feature. Tag feature with an at symbol. It's so intuitive.
ideal: I love this @feature. It's so intuitive.

input: Email john.smith about the deadline. At symbol before john dot smith. Also mention the budget to finance. At symbol before finance too.
ideal: Email @john.smith about the deadline. Also mention the budget to @finance.

input: The function calculateTotal needs refactoring. Add an at symbol before calculateTotal. Actually wait, put an at before function as well.
ideal: The @function @calculateTotal needs refactoring.

input: Slack me later. Tag me with an at symbol. And remind everyone in engineering. At symbol before engineering.
ideal: Slack @me later. And remind everyone in @engineering.

input: The import statement is broken. Add an at symbol before import. Then check the class definition. At symbol before class.
ideal: The @import statement is broken. Then check the @class definition.

input: The React component needs useState imported. At symbol before useState. And don't forget to add the hook dependency array.
ideal: The React component needs @useState imported. And don't forget to add the hook dependency array.

input: Post this in the general channel. Tag general with an at symbol. Then cross-post to announcements and tag announcements with an at symbol too.
ideal: Post this in the @general channel. Then cross-post to @announcements too.

input: The Vue directive v-if needs to be v-show instead. At symbol before v-show. And add the transition wrapper while you're at it.
ideal: The Vue directive v-if needs to be @v-show instead. And add the transition wrapper while you're at it.

input: Mention the backend team in your PR. Tag backend-team with an at symbol. Also tag devops and tag devops with an at symbol for the deployment part.
ideal: Mention the @backend-team in your PR. Also tag @devops for the deployment part.

input: The Twitter handle techcrunch should be in the article. At symbol before techcrunch. And link to their profile page.
ideal: The Twitter handle @techcrunch should be in the article. And link to their profile page.

input: I need to reference the GitHub user octocat in my documentation. Tag octocat with an at symbol. Actually, do it twice since I'm mentioning them in two different sections.
ideal: I need to reference the GitHub user @octocat in my documentation. Actually, do it twice since I'm mentioning them in two different sections.

input: Post this in the general channel and tag everyone. At symbol before general. Also put an at before everyone so they get notified. Make sure it's urgent.
ideal: Post this in the @general channel and tag @everyone. Make sure it's urgent.

input: Email jane.doe and cc her manager. Put an at before jane.doe. Also at symbol before manager but only if you know their handle, otherwise just write the email normally.
ideal: Email @jane.doe and cc her manager. Also @manager but only if you know their handle, otherwise just write the email normally.

input: The Slack workflow needs to notify the on-call engineer and post to incidents. Tag on-call with an at symbol. Also add an at symbol before incidents. Make it high priority.
ideal: The Slack workflow needs to notify the @on-call engineer and post to @incidents. Make it high priority.

input: The Discord bot should respond when someone mentions the admin role or the moderator role. At symbol before admin. Also tag moderator with an at symbol. And make it case insensitive.
ideal: The Discord bot should respond when someone mentions the @admin role or the @moderator role. And make it case insensitive.

input: Hey everyone, check out this new feature from our design team. Tag design with an at symbol. They're doing amazing work.
ideal: Hey everyone, check out this new feature from our @design team. They're doing amazing work.

input: The React component imports useState and useEffect from the library. Add an at symbol before useState and tag useEffect with an at symbol too.
ideal: The React component imports @useState and @useEffect from the library.
